name: Osso Buco with Saffron Risotto Fennel, Red On
recipeIngredient: 2 Carrots, peeled and cut into 2-inch pieces
2 Onions, peeled and cut into 2-inch pieces
1 Zucchini, peeled and cut into 2-inch cubes
1 Yellow squash, peeled and cut into 2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat oven to 325 degrees.
2. Heat olive oil and butter in a small, thick-bottomed rondeau over medium-high heat.
3. Season veal shanks with salt and pepper. Coat with flour, shaking off excess, then brown well.
4. Remove veal shanks from pan. Add vegetables and sachet to the pan, stirring to coat.
5. Place veal shanks on top of the vegetables. Add wine, tomato sauce, and stock; adjust seasoning and bring mixture to a boil.
6. Cover and cook veal shanks in oven for 1 1/2 hours, or until very tender.
7. Start risotto:
- In a large sauce pot over medium heat, sweat diced onion, shallots, and minced garlic in olive oil.
- When vegetables are soft, add Arborio rice and saffron; stir and cook to toast the rice.
- Add white wine, stirring constantly. Stir in chicken stock in three batches until fully absorbed.
8. Continue cooking risotto by adding hot stock to rice in thirds, stirring until liquid is absorbed.
9. Finish osso buco:
- Remove shanks from rondeau and reduce jus to desired consistency.
- Make tomato fondue: Heat olive oil in a sauce pot over medium heat. Add onions and cook until soft.
- Season with salt and pepper; add garlic, cooking briefly without browning.
- Add tomatoes and reduce liquid slightly thickened.
10. Prepare gremolata:
- Combine chopped parsley, garlic, lemon zest in a bowl.
11. Make salad:
- Peel orange and remove segments. Squeeze juice over segments.
- Shave fennel, onion with mandolin; add to mixing bowl with remaining ingredients.
- Toss all together and serve with risotto and osso buco.
12. Fold butter and cheese into risotto; keep warm.
13. Pour jus over veal shanks, top with gremolata, and serve with saffron risotto.
